accurate.why: All claims align with the bill text and accurate context (DHS as administering department, statute citations) is correct.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; figures, programs, and exclusions match the bill text.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill as supplemental DHS appropriations for border security and immigration enforcement.

changes_to_existing_law.why: References 287(g) agreements, information-sharing laws, and Public Law 119-21 consistent with the bill's references.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes the processing coordinator restriction, surveillance tower restriction, tribal exemption, and release prohibition.

effective_dates.why: Includes the September 30, 2029 availability and the October 31, 2028 hiring restriction date.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures all major appropriations across both titles with correct amounts and purposes.

objective_tone.why: Summary is neutral and descriptive without editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized by title and reads clearly.

concise.why: Avoids padding and filler while covering key provisions.

correct_entities.why: CBP, ICE, HSI, DHS, and statutory references are all correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: All dollar amounts and dates ($9.55B, $7.45B, $108.5M, $3.45B, $13.02B, $31.075B, $350M, $2.5B, dates) match the bill.
